data.title: Fold Adds 475 BTC, Secures Top 10 Spot in U.S. Public Bitcoin Treasuries
data.description:
• Fold’s bitcoin treasury grows by nearly 50%, reaching over 1,485 BTC after acquiring 475 BTC through a convertible note.
  • Secures a top 10 position among U.S. public companies with the largest bitcoin holdings, reinforcing its commitment to bitcoin-native financial services.

data.title: The Fourth Largest Weekly Drop in Dollar Index in Over a Decade Signals Bitcoin Bottom
data.description:
• Fourth occurrence of a -4 standard deviation drop in the DXY Index since 2013, a rare event historically linked to bitcoin bottoms.
  • Previous instances in 2015, 2020, and 2022 led to significant bitcoin rebounds following the drop.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ether, Solana Traders Chase Downside Protection, XRP Stands Out, as Trump's Crypto Plan Disappoints
data.description:
• Crypto traders chase short-dated puts tied to BTC, ETH, SOL.
  • XRP options show bias for bullish calls across time frames, except one.
  • Some observers pin hope on Friday's crypto summit and payrolls to support risk assets.

data.title: Coinbase Acquires Iron Fish Team to Boost Privacy to Boost Privacy on Base
data.description:
• Coinbase acquired the team behind Iron Fish, a privacy-focused blockchain project, to enhance privacy features on its Layer 2 network Base.
  • The Iron Fish blockchain and its native token will continue to operate independently under the Iron Fish Foundation.
  • Coinbase aims to offer developers tools to build more secure and compliant decentralized applications by integrating privacy features into Base.

data.title: U.S. Stocks Give Up Post-Trump Election Advance While Bitcoin Clings to Gain
data.description:
• Both the Nasdaq 100 and S&P 500 have erased all gains since Donald Trump's November election victory.
  • While giving up a substantial portion of its advance, bitcoin is still about 20% higher since Trump won the presidency.

data.title: XRP, DOGE, ADA Slump 9% as Trump Douses Bullish Crypto Reserve Hopes
data.description:
• Major cryptocurrencies including XRP, DOGE, and ADA saw a drop of up to 9% after President Trump's decision to establish a Bitcoin Strategic Reserve, excluding these tokens.
  • The decision led to over $400 million in liquidations on bullish bets in crypto futures, primarily from BTC-tracked products.
  • Traders are now focusing on the upcoming White House Crypto Summit, though expectations are low due to the lack of substantial promises from Trump.

data.title: Market Experts Weigh In on Trump’s Strategic Bitcoin Reserve That Takes Out $17B in Potential Selling From BTC
data.description:
• U.S. President Donald Trump has signed an executive order to establish a strategic bitcoin reserve, which will include BTC seized by the U.S. government through law enforcement actions.
  • The U.S. government currently holds 198,000 bitcoins worth about $17.3 billion, and this move essentially removes over $17 billion in selling pressure from the market.
  • Opinions about the reserve are mixed with some calling it a symbolic move while others hailing it as unambiguously positive.

data.title: Here Is What Market Pundits Are Saying About Trump’s Strategic Bitcoin Announcement
data.description:
• U.S. President Donald Trump has signed an executive order to establish a strategic bitcoin reserve, which will include BTC seized by the U.S. government through law enforcement actions.
  • The U.S. government currently holds 198,000 bitcoins worth about $17.3 billion, and this move essentially removes over $17 billion in selling pressure from the market.
  • Opinions about the reserve are mixed with some calling it a symbolic move while others hailing it as unambiguously positive.

data.title: Rate Fears Replace Tariff Fears as Crypto Pulls Back
data.description:
• Risk markets sank on Thursday even as the White House walked back its latest tariff threat.
  • Interest rates are rising across the globe, another possible headwind for bitcoin and crypto.
  • Tomorrow's U.S. jobs report has likely taken on added significance.

data.title: Tokenized Asset Manager Superstate Registers Transfer Agent with SEC
data.description:
• Blockchain-based asset management firm Superstate has registered its digital transfer agent, Superstate Services LLC, with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to bridge tokenized assets with existing financial regulations.
  • Superstate Services will use blockchain-integrated record-keeping to track ownership in real time and a smart contract-driven allowlist to ensure only approved investors can acquire tokenized shares.
  • The registration follows SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce highlighting the importance of transfer agents in the future of tokenized securities in .
